commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0ceb0e289684e48fdec786f8a464864c1cdea87b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `text_end`, `thinking_end`, `toolcall_start`, and `toolcall_end` to the opencode-go stalled-stream liveness predicate.

PR surface: Source +4. Total +4 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main forwards boundary events but only re-arms idle liveness for deltas, and the linked issue includes a focused failing timing test; I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human PR review and contributor action because the branch has a concrete timing defect and the external real-behavior-proof gate cannot be satisfied by an automated repair marker.

target.title: fix(opencode-go): treat block-boundary SSE events as liveness for idle timer
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The opencode-go stalled-stream watchdog can abort a stream that is still alive and actively producing output, replacing a completed answer with a stalled error. When the model finalizes a tool call and deliberates before the next one, the provider emits real block-boundary SSE events (`text_end`, `thinking_end`, `toolcall_start`, `toolcall_end`) that prove the socket is alive, but the watchdog does not treat them as liveness signals. If the gap between two consecutive token deltas exceeds `idleTimeoutMs` while these boundary events keep flowing, the watchdog calls `controller.abort()` and pushes a terminal stalled error, and the real `done` is silently dropped.

This affects all opencode-go models (Kimi, GLM, MiniMax, DeepSeek) and is most likely to trigger on cron coding turns with long tool-call deliberation.

## Why This Change Was Made

The idle re-arm predicate `isProviderProgressEvent` in `extensions/opencode-go/stream-termination.ts:54` only returned true for `text_delta`, `thinking_delta`, and `toolcall_delta`. Block-boundary events were forwarded to the consumer but did not re-arm the idle timer, contradicting the wrapper docstring which states "provider progress refreshes the idle timer."

The fix adds `text_end`, `thinking_end`, `toolcall_start`, and `toolcall_end` to the predicate, aligning the code with the documented contract.

## Changes

- **`extensions/opencode-go/stream-termination.ts`** — added `text_end`, `thinking_end`, `toolcall_start`, and `toolcall_end` to `isProviderProgressEvent` so these block-boundary SSE events re-arm the idle timer

## Evidence

### Test command
```
npx vitest run extensions/opencode-go/ --reporter=verbose
```

### Test output
```
Test Files  5 passed (5)
Tests  27 passed (27)
Duration  45.84s
```

Key passing tests in `stream-termination.test.ts`:
- aborts underlying stream when progress stalls after first delta
- uses a longer first-event timeout than the inter-event idle timeout
- keeps the first-event window after an openai-completions synthetic start
- keeps the first-event window after synthetic block-start events until a provider delta
- aborts and releases the underlying stream when no first event arrives
- preserves normal delayed usage-only completion without aborting

### Behavior verification
- Before: boundary events between deltas did not re-arm the idle timer, causing false-positive stalled-stream aborts
- After: boundary events refresh the idle timer alongside deltas, preventing premature abort of live streams

## Related

Closes #96518
